diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Public Relations from early publicity to purpose‑driven global practice. Key milestones: early publicity tactics and press bureaus (1900s); Ivy Lee’s “Declaration of Principles” (1906) establishing openness and truth; Edward Bernays’s seminal works and first textbook (Crystallizing Public Opinion, 1923); the post‑WWII expansion of corporate PR (1950s‑60s); Grunig and Hunt’s four models of PR (1984); the digital revolution of the late 1990s with online newsrooms and email; the rise of social media and content marketing (2000s‑2010s); the emergence of influencer relations, data‑driven measurement, and reputation analytics; and the 2020s focus on ESG, purpose‑driven communication, AI integration, and real‑time engagement.
